    {
      "study_date": "2022-10-28",
      "procedure": "MR Abdomen Without Contrast",
      "history": "HCC",
      "findings": "Postsurgical changes of a segment 5/6 resection. The liver is overall normal in morphology with normal signal intensity and homogeneous postcontrast enhancement. Within segment 7 of the liver is a new 1.6 cm T2 hyperintense lesion (6#12) which is not well seen on the arterial phase of imaging, but demonstrates washout with capsule on the portal venous phase of imaging (13#13, 14#13). The diffusion images are nondiagnostic. This lesion is worrisome for a new focus of hepatocellular carcinoma. In addition, best seen on the T2 weighted images, series 6, are multifocal subcentimeter arterial enhancing lesions, for example series 6#16, 17, 27, 29. A few of these demonstrate arterial phase enhancement with washout, for example on series 12, #47, with corresponding washout on series 13#49 and 46. Spleen is not enlarged. No ascites and no varices. Pancreas is normal in morphology. Normal caliber pancreatic duct. Status post cholecystectomy. No intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation. The right adrenal gland and kidneys are grossly unremarkable in appearance. Nodular thickening of the left adrenal gland which loses signal on out of phase imaging, likely adenomatous hyperplasia. Abdominal aorta has a normal course and caliber. No enlarged lymph nodes within the abdomen. The hepatic, portal, splenic and superior mesenteric veins are patent.",
      "impression": "1. Status post segment 5/6 resection. Within segment 7 of the liver is a new 1.6 cm lesion which demonstrates washout with capsule, and T2 hyperintense signal. This is not seen on the arterial phase of imaging, although favored to be technical. This is worrisome for new focus of disease. 2. Additional scattered observations within the right lobe of the liver demonstrating T2 hyperintense signal, some which demonstrate arterial enhancement and washout. These findings are worrisome for multifocal hepatocellular carcinoma. PLEASE NOTE: Our interpretation of studies performed at an outside institution is limited by factors including the absence of technical specifics of the image, undisclosed clinical information and the unavailability of the original interpretation. Specialists at the institution that performed the study may have access to information not available to us that could make a difference in this interpretation. We suggest that you obtain the original interpretation from the site where the study was performed."

There are few scattered subcentimeter isolated foci of arterial phase hyperenhancement in right hepatic lobe hepatic lobe (series 801, image 139, image 116, image 108) without signal abnormality on additional sequences. There is a small focus of arterial phase hyperenhancement with associated impeded effusion in segment 7, 0.8 cm (series 801, image 126 series 301, image 385). GALLBLADDER: Prior cholecystectomy. BILIARY: Mild postcholecystectomy reservoir effect. PANCREAS: No solid mass or ductal dilation. SPLEEN: No splenomegaly. ADRENAL GLANDS: Unchanged lipid rich left adrenal gland thickening. Unremarkable right adrenal gland. KIDNEYS: No collecting system dilatation. Tiny cyst in the right kidney. BOWEL: No abnormal bowel thickening or dilatation. Consider attention on follow-up. 2. Small focus of arterial phase hyperenhancement with associated impeded effusion in segment 7 may represent residual untreated tumor (0.8 cm). 3. There are additional scattered foci of isolated arterial phase hyperenhancement which may represent perfusional alteration versus additional foci of residual tumor."
